results.possibleIdentity: Edies Harris (also identified as Edie Harris) was a technical professional and computer programmer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s. Harris was a member of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to facilitate collaborations between the artistic and engineering communities. According to technical index records from E.A.T., Harris was employed by International Business Machines (IBM) in New York City, specializing in the field of data processing programming. By the mid-1980s, an Edie Harris was noted as an instructor at the Software Institute of America, where she designed efficient instructional approaches for data processing and software applications such as Lotus 1-2-3.
